1036.reason: If semicolon is missing, nextIndex is not advanced. The caller (parseBlock) will loop infinitely on the same token because the error recovery does not skip to the next statement or synchronize.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If semicolon is missing, nextIndex is not advanced. The caller (parseBlock) will loop infinitely on the same token because the error recovery does not skip to the next statement or synchronize.

1040.reason: If '=' is missing, nextIndex is not advanced. This leads to an infinite loop in the caller.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If '=' is missing, nextIndex is not advanced. This leads to an infinite loop in the caller.

1050.reason: [Error: If parseExp() fails to consume tokens (e.g., due to syntax error inside expression) or if the current token is not SEMICN and parseExp didn't advance, nextIndex remains unchanged. This causes the caller (parseBlock) to loop infinitely on the same token.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If semicolon is missing, nextIndex is not advanced. This leads to an infinite loop in the caller (parseBlock).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: If parseExp() fails to consume tokens (e.g., due to syntax error inside expression) or if the current token is not SEMICN and parseExp didn't advance, nextIndex remains unchanged. This causes the caller (parseBlock) to loop infinitely on the same token.]

971.reason: [This should report error for missing ')' in printf, but the line number calculation may be incorrect because tokens.get(nextIndex - 1) might not correspond to the actual last valid token before the missing parenthesis]
